sections.title: Types of Bone Fractures
points: Simple: A clean break where the bone stays inside the flesh.
Compound: The bone pierces the skin. It is dangerous due to heavy bleeding and high risk of infection.
Greenstick: The bone bends or cracks but doesn't snap. Common in children because their bones are flexible.
Comminuted: The bone is shattered or crushed into many small pieces.

sections.title: Muscle Cramp & Fatigue
points: Cramp: A sudden, painful, and involuntary contraction of a muscle.
Causes: Dehydration (lack of water), Lactic acid buildup, or extreme muscle fatigue.
First Aid: Gently stretch and massage the cramped muscle.

sections.title: First Aid Mastery
points: Splint: A stiff support used to immobilize a limb. It prevents broken bone ends from cutting nerves and blood vessels.
Arm Sling: A triangular bandage used to take the weight off a broken arm or shoulder.
Stretcher: Used to carry casualties with spinal injuries. Keeping the back straight prevents the broken vertebrae from severing the spinal cord.

sections.text: The R.I.C.E. Rule for Joint Injuries: Rest the part, Ice the swelling, Compress with a bandage, Elevate the limb above the heart!

cards.fact: A complete or partial break in a bone.
Waste product that causes muscle fatigue/cramps.
When a bone is forced out of its socket at a joint.
cards.mnemonic: The bone has been Dis-Located (put in the wrong place)!
